LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ERIC SAFIRE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. FIRST, THE SPECIAL DEMURRERS (UNCERTAINTY, CANNOT ASCERTAIN TYPE OF CONTRACT) ARE IMPROPER BECAUSE THIS IS A LIMITED JURISDICTION CASE. (CCP 92(C)). SECOND, THE ONLY CAUSE OF ACTION ASSERTED AGAINST THE MOVING DEFENDANT (MR. SAFIRE) IS FOR COMMON COUNTS. THE ONLY ESSENTIAL ALLEGATIONS ARE (1) THE STATEMENT OF INDEBTEDNESS IN A CERTAIN SUM, (2) THE CONSIDERATION, AND (3) NONPAYMENT. SEE WITKIN, CAL. PROCEDURE, PLEADING 553, 557. HERE, PLAINTIFF MAKES THOSE ALLEGATIONS AND THE COURT LIBERALLY CONSTRUES THE COMPLAINT PER CCP 452. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/EG

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-02-2016 Vacated

Ntc And Mtn For Court Judgment Be Default Prove Up
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840

Plaintiff, Michael S. Keck, appearing on his own behalf, sworn and examined,
No appearance by defaulted defendant Kimberly Coleman-Curry,
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:
Damages: $10,969.50
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0.00
Attorney's Fees: $0.00
Costs: $800.32
Total Judgment in the amount of $11,769.82

Judgment signed in open court.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-07-2016 Set For Trial On May-23-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-12-2015 Continued To Jan-26-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2015 Set For Order To Show Cause On Nov-17-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT ERIC SAFIRE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. First, the special demurrers (uncertainty, cannot ascertain type of contract) are improper because this is a limited jurisdiction case. (CCP 92(c)). Second, the only cause of action asserted against the moving defendant (Mr. Safire) is for common counts. The only essential allegations are (1) the statement of indebtedness in a certain sum, (2) the consideration, and (3) nonpayment. See Witkin, Cal. Procedure, Pleading 553, 557. Here, plaintiff makes those allegations and the court liberally construes the complaint per CCP 452. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
